name: spec-kit-001-reservation-approval-context-pack

goals: FR-001: The system MUST approve a reservation only when stock is available.
FR-002: The system MUST persist the approval actor and approval timestamp.
FR-003: The system MUST reject approval when available stock is lower than the requested quantity.
non_goals: Do not vendor or fork GitHub Spec Kit.
Do not require Spec Kit for normal ae-framework usage.
Do not treat report-only bridge findings as merge approval.

terms.ja: Spec Kit 取込フィーチャ
Spec Kit 連携ブリッジ
terms.note: Report-only mapping into Context Pack and ExecPlan v2 references.

pre: Spec Kit feature directory is readable.
post: Requirement, plan, task, contract, and verification references are preserved as report-only assurance inputs.

pre: Submit a reservation request with sufficient stock and confirm the approval event is recorded.
post: Given sufficient stock, When an operator approves the reservation, Then the reservation status becomes approved.
Given an approved reservation, When evidence is rendered, Then the approval actor and timestamp are visible.

pre: Submit a reservation request with insufficient stock and confirm no approval event is emitted.
post: Given insufficient stock, When an operator attempts approval, Then the reservation is rejected with an out-of-stock reason.

diagrams.statement: Spec Kit spec.md, plan.md, tasks.md, and contracts/* are imported into Context Pack and ExecPlan v2 without replacing their source artifacts.

acceptance_tests.scenario: The system MUST approve a reservation only when stock is available.
expected: SC-001: Approval and rejection scenarios are covered by automated tests.
SC-002: Review artifacts link requirements, tasks, and contracts before merge judgment.

acceptance_tests.scenario: The system MUST persist the approval actor and approval timestamp.
expected: SC-001: Approval and rejection scenarios are covered by automated tests.
SC-002: Review artifacts link requirements, tasks, and contracts before merge judgment.

acceptance_tests.scenario: The system MUST reject approval when available stock is lower than the requested quantity.
expected: SC-001: Approval and rejection scenarios are covered by automated tests.
SC-002: Review artifacts link requirements, tasks, and contracts before merge judgment.
Given sufficient stock, When an operator approves the reservation, Then the reservation status becomes approved.
Given an approved reservation, When evidence is rendered, Then the approval actor and timestamp are visible.
Given insufficient stock, When an operator attempts approval, Then the reservation is rejected with an out-of-stock reason.

forbidden_changes: Do not vendor or fork GitHub Spec Kit inside ae-framework.
Do not promote bridge findings from report-only to blocking without a separate policy issue.
Do not drop source Spec Kit artifact paths when rendering assurance evidence.
